title: Zoom adds Agent Architect and performance tracking to Zoom Virtual Agent
friendlyTitle: Zoom adds tools to build and measure customer-service AI agents
summary: Zoom announced new Zoom Virtual Agent capabilities including Agent Architect, Agent Performance Suite, quality management for AI interactions, an enhanced customer context layer, multi-location deployments, and optional outcome-based pricing. The updates focus on building agents, simulating and measuring performance, and tying costs to resolved or successfully routed interactions.
friendlySummary: Zoom added features for creating voice and chat agents, testing them, measuring results, and pricing some usage by successful outcomes.
whyItMatters: The contact-center market is starting to measure agents by outcomes instead of deployment counts. For businesses, this makes resolution rate, containment, cost per resolution, and handoff quality central buying criteria for AI support systems.
friendlyWhyItMatters: AI support agents should be judged by whether they solve customer problems. Better measurement helps companies avoid deploying bots that only look impressive.
